title: Safety & Methods
description: Keep your arrays clean and type-safe.

slides.title: The Array Bouncer 🛡️
slides.content: Once an array is typed as a `string[]`, TypeScript acts as a bouncer, constantly checking the array to ensure no non-strings manage to sneak in.

slides.title: Guarding Methods
slides.content: If you try to `.push()` the wrong type into the array, TypeScript throws an error instantly.

quizData.question: What happens when you extract a value from `players: string[]` via `let p = players[0];`?

options.text: TypeScript has no idea what 'p' is, so it types it as 'any'.

quizData.explanation: Because TS knows the array only contains strings, pulling an item out assigns it the `string` type safely.

slides.title: Better Map and Filter 🗺️
slides.content:
This safety cascades down to array methods like `.map()` and `.filter()`.

When mapping over `players: string[]`, TS perfectly knows the anonymous callback parameters are `string`s, granting you awesome autocomplete.

slides.title: Method Inference
slides.content: No need to type `player` here; TS already knows it's a string!
